journey.name: Login & Dashboard Flow
journey.description: End-to-end customer journey covering registration validation, authentication, profile management, and session lifecycle.

steps.name: Attempt duplicate registration
steps.description: Try to register an account with an email that already exists. Validates the API correctly rejects duplicates with a 409 Conflict response.

body.error: An account with this email already exists.

expected.description: 409 Conflict — the email is already registered. Response body contains an error message. No loyalty number is returned.
expected.validationNotes: Confirms idempotency guard on registration. The existing account is not modified.

steps.name: Login
steps.description: Authenticate with valid credentials to obtain a JWT access token, refresh token, and the customer's loyalty number.

expected.description: 200 OK — credentials validated. Response contains a short-lived JWT access token (15 min TTL), a single-use refresh token, expiry timestamp, token type, and the customer's loyalty number.
expected.validationNotes: accessToken is a signed JWT (RS256). refreshToken is opaque and single-use. loyaltyNumber starts with 'AX' and is 9 characters. expiresAt is ISO 8601 UTC, ~15 minutes from now.

steps.name: Customer search (direct microservice)
steps.description: Direct call to the Customer microservice Search endpoint. Finds the customer record by loyalty number to retrieve the identity ID needed for account unlock.

apiCall.note: Direct microservice call — not routed through the Loyalty orchestration API

expected.description: 200 OK — returns an array of matching customer records. The identityId field links to the Identity microservice account.
expected.validationNotes: Response is always an array (empty if no match). identityId is a GUID that maps to the UserAccountId in the Identity MS. Use this for the unlock call in step 3b.

steps.name: Verify email / unlock account (direct microservice)
steps.description: Direct call to the Identity microservice to verify the account's email address, effectively unlocking it for first-time login. This simulates the email verification step that completes registration.

apiCall.note: Direct microservice call — uses identityId from step 3a as the userAccountId path parameter

expected.description: 200 OK — email verified, account unlocked. No response body.
expected.validationNotes: Idempotent — calling again on an already-verified account still returns 200. A 404 means the identityId was not found.

steps.name: Token refresh
steps.description: Exchange the single-use refresh token from login for a new access token and refresh token pair. The old refresh token is revoked.

expected.description: 200 OK — new token pair issued. The previous refresh token is now invalid (single-use rotation).
expected.validationNotes: New accessToken replaces the one from step 2. New refreshToken must be used for any subsequent refresh. Old refreshToken from step 2 is revoked and cannot be reused.

steps.name: View profile
steps.description: Retrieve the authenticated customer's loyalty profile using the loyalty number from login and the refreshed access token.

expected.description: 200 OK — full profile returned. Tier defaults to 'Blue' for new members. Points balance starts at 0.
expected.validationNotes: loyaltyNumber matches the value from login. tier is one of: Blue, Silver, Gold, Platinum. pointsBalance is a decimal. memberSince is ISO 8601 UTC.

steps.name: View transactions
steps.description: Retrieve the transaction history for the authenticated customer. Shows point earn/redeem activity with pagination.

expected.description: 200 OK — paginated transaction history. Each transaction includes type, point delta (positive for earn, negative for redeem), running balance, and optional booking/flight references.
expected.validationNotes: transactionType is one of: Earn, Redeem, Adjustment, Expiry, Reinstate. pointsDelta is positive for credits, negative for debits. balanceAfter is the running balance after each transaction. A new account may return an empty transactions array with totalCount 0.

steps.name: Update profile
steps.description: Patch the customer's profile with updated fields. Only the provided fields are modified; omitted fields remain unchanged.

expected.description: 204 No Content — profile updated successfully. No response body. A subsequent GET /profile would reflect the changes.
expected.validationNotes: Only permitted fields are accepted: givenName, surname, dateOfBirth, nationality, phoneNumber, preferredLanguage. Fields like tier, points, identityId are ignored. Partial update — omitted fields are not cleared.

steps.name: Logout
steps.description: Invalidate the session by revoking the refresh token. The access token remains valid until its TTL expires (~15 min).

expected.description: 200 OK — refresh token revoked. Session effectively terminated. The access token is still technically valid until its 15-minute TTL expires, but no new tokens can be obtained.
expected.validationNotes: Attempting to use the revoked refresh token for a subsequent /auth/refresh call should return 401 Unauthorized.
